title: 151. Which is the oldest National Park in India?
hint: It was originally called Hailey National Park.
summary: Jim Corbett National Park, established in 1936, is the oldest national park in India.

title: 152. Gir National Park is famous for conserving which animal?
hint: It is the only wild population of this species in Asia.
summary: Gir National Park in Gujarat is the last natural habitat of the Asiatic Lion.

title: 153. Kaziranga National Park is located in which state?
hint: The Brahmaputra River flows through this state.
summary: Kaziranga National Park is located in Assam.

title: 154. Which National Park is famous for the one-horned rhinoceros?
hint: It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in Assam.
summary: Kaziranga National Park is globally known for the Indian one-horned rhinoceros.

title: 155. Sundarbans National Park is known for which ecosystem?
hint: Trees here grow in salty coastal water.
summary: Sundarbans National Park contains the world's largest mangrove ecosystem.

title: 156. Which National Park is located in Rajasthan and famous for tiger sightings?
hint: It contains a historic fort within the park.
summary: Ranthambore National Park is one of India's most famous tiger reserves and national parks.

title: 157. Keoladeo National Park is especially famous for which group of animals?
hint: Bird watchers from around the world visit here.
summary: Keoladeo National Park is one of the world's most important bird habitats.

title: 158. Hemis National Park is located in which Union Territory?
hint: It is known for snow leopards.
summary: Hemis National Park is located in Ladakh.

title: 159. Which National Park is famous for hangul (Kashmir stag)?
hint: Located near Srinagar.
summary: Dachigam National Park is the primary habitat of the endangered hangul.

title: 160. Great Himalayan National Park is located in which state?
hint: Its capital is Shimla.
summary: Great Himalayan National Park is located in Himachal Pradesh.

title: 161. Which National Park in Gujarat is famous for Asiatic lions?
hint: It is the only natural habitat of Asiatic lions.
summary: Gir National Park is the last natural home of Asiatic lions.

title: 162. Which National Park is India's first Marine National Park?
hint: Located in the Gulf of Kachchh.
summary: Marine National Park in Gujarat was India's first marine national park.

title: 163. Which National Park is famous for the floating meadows called Phumdis?
hint: Located in Manipur.
summary: Keibul Lamjao National Park contains floating vegetation called phumdis.

title: 164. Which endangered deer is found in Keibul Lamjao National Park?
hint: Also known as the dancing deer.
summary: Sangai deer is endemic to the Loktak Lake region of Manipur.

title: 165. Which National Park is located in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands and known for coral reefs?
hint: Named after Mahatma Gandhi.
summary: Mahatma Gandhi Marine National Park protects coral reefs and marine biodiversity.

title: 166. Which National Park in Madhya Pradesh inspired Rudyard Kipling's 'The Jungle Book'?
hint: It is famous for barasingha conservation.
summary: Kanha National Park is believed to have inspired the setting of The Jungle Book.

title: 167. Bandhavgarh National Park is located in which state?
hint: The state is known as the Tiger State of India.
summary: Bandhavgarh National Park is located in Madhya Pradesh.

title: 168. Which National Park is famous for high tiger density and an ancient hill fort?
hint: Located in Madhya Pradesh.
summary: Bandhavgarh National Park is known for one of the highest tiger densities in India.

title: 169. Pench National Park is spread across which two states?
hint: Both states are in central India.
summary: Pench National Park lies in Madhya Pradesh and Maharashtra.

title: 170. Which National Park became famous due to the reintroduction of cheetahs in India?
hint: Located in Madhya Pradesh.
summary: Kuno National Park became the site of India's cheetah reintroduction project.

title: 171. Which National Park is located in the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve in Karnataka?
hint: It shares boundaries with Mudumalai and Wayanad.
summary: Bandipur National Park is part of the Nilgiri Biosphere Reserve.

title: 172. Nagarhole National Park is located in which state?
hint: Its capital city is Bengaluru.
summary: Nagarhole National Park is located in Karnataka.

title: 173. Which National Park is famous for lion-tailed macaques in Kerala?
hint: Located in the Western Ghats.
summary: Silent Valley National Park protects one of the last undisturbed tropical rainforests in India.

title: 174. Eravikulam National Park is famous for which endangered species?
hint: A mountain goat found in the Western Ghats.
summary: Eravikulam National Park is the largest habitat of the Nilgiri Tahr.

title: 175. Which National Park in Kerala is famous for the blooming of Neelakurinji flowers every 12 years?
hint: It is also known for Nilgiri Tahr.
summary: Eravikulam National Park is renowned for the periodic blooming of Neelakurinji flowers.

title: 176. Which National Park in Assam is also a UNESCO World Heritage Site and Tiger Reserve?
hint: It is famous for one-horned rhinoceroses.
summary: Kaziranga National Park is both a UNESCO World Heritage Site and a Tiger Reserve.

title: 177. Manas National Park is located along the border with which country?
hint: The neighboring country's national animal is the takin.
summary: Manas National Park shares a border with Bhutan.

title: 178. Which National Park is known as India's only floating National Park?
hint: Located on Loktak Lake.
summary: Keibul Lamjao National Park is the world's only floating national park.

title: 179. Which National Park is the largest National Park in India by area?
hint: Located in Ladakh.
summary: Hemis National Park is the largest National Park in India.

title: 180. Which National Park is located in Sikkim and forms part of a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
hint: It includes India's highest mountain.
summary: Khangchendzonga National Park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

title: 181. Desert National Park is located in which state?
hint: The Thar Desert dominates this state.
summary: Desert National Park is located in Rajasthan.

title: 182. Which critically endangered bird is associated with Desert National Park?
hint: It is one of the heaviest flying birds in the world.
summary: The Great Indian Bustard is strongly associated with Desert National Park.

title: 183. Valley of Flowers National Park is located in which state?
hint: It is part of the western Himalayas.
summary: Valley of Flowers National Park is located in Uttarakhand.

title: 184. Which National Park is paired with Valley of Flowers as a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
hint: A famous Sikh pilgrimage site is nearby.
summary: Nanda Devi National Park and Valley of Flowers form a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

title: 185. Which National Park contains the world's largest mangrove forest ecosystem?
hint: It lies in a delta formed by the Ganga, Brahmaputra and Meghna rivers.
summary: Sundarbans National Park contains the world's largest mangrove ecosystem.

title: 186. Which National Park is known for saltwater crocodiles and olive ridley turtles in Odisha?
hint: It lies near the Brahmani and Baitarani river delta.
summary: Bhitarkanika National Park is famous for estuarine crocodiles.

title: 187. Namdapha National Park is located in which state?
hint: India's easternmost state with very high biodiversity.
summary: Namdapha National Park is located in Arunachal Pradesh.

title: 188. Which National Park is famous for all four major big cat species being reported from its landscape?
hint: Located in Arunachal Pradesh.
summary: Namdapha National Park is famous for exceptional big cat diversity.

title: 189. Which National Park protects the highest peak entirely within India?
hint: The peak is Nanda Devi.
summary: Nanda Devi National Park protects the Nanda Devi massif.

title: 190. Which National Park is famous for snow leopard conservation in Ladakh?
hint: It is India's largest National Park.
summary: Hemis National Park is one of the best places in the world to observe snow leopards.

title: 191. Which National Park in Tamil Nadu is known for the endangered Nilgiri Tahr and is part of the Western Ghats UNESCO World Heritage Site?
hint: It is located near Ooty and Coonoor.
summary: Mukurthi National Park protects montane grasslands and the endangered Nilgiri Tahr.

title: 192. Guindy National Park is unique because it is located within which major Indian city?
hint: This city is the capital of Tamil Nadu.
summary: Guindy National Park is one of the few national parks located inside a major metropolitan city.

title: 193. Which National Park in Maharashtra is famous for its tiger reserve and bamboo forests?
hint: It shares a landscape with Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve.
summary: Tadoba National Park forms the core of Tadoba-Andhari Tiger Reserve.

title: 194. Sanjay Gandhi National Park is located in which metropolitan city?
hint: India's financial capital.
summary: Sanjay Gandhi National Park is located within Mumbai city limits.

title: 195. Which National Park in the Andaman and Nicobar Islands contains Saddle Peak, the highest point of the islands?
hint: The peak gives the park its identity.
summary: Saddle Peak National Park contains the highest point in the Andaman Islands.

title: 196. Campbell Bay National Park is located on which island?
hint: India's southernmost large island.
summary: Campbell Bay National Park is situated on Great Nicobar Island.

title: 197. Sri Venkateswara National Park is located in which state?
hint: The famous Tirumala Hills are located within this protected area.
summary: Sri Venkateswara National Park is located in Andhra Pradesh and forms part of the Eastern Ghats ecosystem.
questionTags: National Parks,Sri Venkateswara National Park,Andhra Pradesh,Eastern Ghats,UPSC,RPSC

title: 198. Which National Park is famous for red panda conservation in the Eastern Himalayas?
hint: Located in Sikkim.
summary: Khangchendzonga National Park provides habitat for the red panda.

title: 199. Which National Park in West Bengal is famous for red pandas and the Singalila Ridge?
hint: It borders Nepal.
summary: Singalila National Park is one of India's most important habitats for red pandas.

title: 200. Which National Park is known as the only National Park in Delhi?
hint: It protects a section of the Aravalli landscape.
summary: Asola Bhatti Wildlife Sanctuary is not a National Park; Delhi currently has no National Park. Sultanpur National Park is in Haryana. Therefore, there is no National Park within Delhi.

options.title: No National Park exists in Delhi
